text: **Professor Bot:** In part one of this two-part {lesson|授業/レッスン}.
**Professor Bot:** Ms.
**Professor Bot:** Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver {instructed|指示した/命じた} them to do everything by themselves.
**Professor Bot:** {themselves|自分自身|再帰代名詞} is a reflexive pronoun.
**Professor Bot:** In today's lesson, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver will look at Pete's and Anna's {research|研究/調査}.
**Anna:** First, let's see Pete's {experiment|実験}.
**Anna:** Okay Pete, let me see your research first.
**Anna:** That's a {heavy|重い} binder.
**Pete:** And you and your baby look great.
**Pete:** Thanks, Miss Weaver.
**Pete:** We feel great.
**Professor Bot:** Anna?
**Anna:** You're next.
**Professor Bot:** Anna.
**Anna:** Anna, wake up.
**Anna:** I'm here, baby.
**Anna:** I'm here.
**Anna:** Here's your {bottle|哺乳瓶}.
**Anna:** Anna, you and your baby look awful.
**Anna:** What happened?
**Anna:** Well, I took her everywhere.
**Anna:** Everywhere.
**Anna:** And I fed her every three hours.
**Anna:** So I haven't slept since what day is it?
**Anna:** It's Friday.
**Anna:** What happened there?
**Anna:** Oh that, oh that.
**Anna:** I was making myself a {salad|サラダ} and had a little {accident|事故/偶発的な出来事} with a knife
**Anna:** I put my {flower|花} baby in front of me, and then I {accidentally|偶然に} {stabbed|刺した/突いた} it, but I gave it first aid
**Anna:** Is that a {burn|やけど}?
**Anna:** Yes.
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Anna:** While I was making {myself|自分自身} dinner
**Anna:** I put baby next to me.
**Anna:** I {accidentally|偶然に} {knocked|叩いた/落とした} her into the sink.
**Anna:** So I put her in the {microwave|電子レンジ} to dry.
**Anna:** That's when she caught {fire|火災}.
**Professor Bot:** Okay, so we use a reflexive pronoun when it refers to the subject of a sentence or clause.
**Professor Bot:** But we don't use a reflexive pronoun with prepositions of place.
**Anna:** Anna uses examples of both in one sentence.
**Anna:** While I was making myself dinner, I put baby next to me.
**Professor Bot:** We use a reflexive pronoun in the first part of the sentence.
**Professor Bot:** Myself refers to the subject I, but in the second part of the sentence
**Professor Bot:** We don't use a reflexive pronoun in the prepositional phrase.
**Professor Bot:** We use the pronoun me.
**Anna:** Why?
**Anna:** Next to is a {preposition|前置詞} of place.
**Professor Bot:** Anna.
**Anna:** Anna, you should be very {proud|誇りに思う} of yourself.
**Pete:** Proud?
**Anna:** She {stabbed|刺した/突いた} and {burned|燃やした} her baby
**Anna:** And she only did one page of {research|調査/研究}, and it's covered in milk.
**Anna:** Ugh!
**Professor Bot:** Yes, Pete.
**Anna:** But she followed {instructions|指示}.
**Pete:** Hey!
**Anna:** I did the Please, Pete.
**Anna:** Anna, I think your baby has lost some {weight|重さ/体重}.
**Anna:** Is there something else you want to {share|共有する/分かち合う}?
**Anna:** Yes, I'd like to share these.
**Anna:** I {made|作った/製造した} them myself this morning.
**Pete:** You {baked|焼いた/調理した} your baby?
**Anna:** You should be {ashamed|恥じる/恥ずかしい} of yourself.
**Anna:** I baked them at the {end|終わり/最後} of the experiment, Pete.
**Anna:** At that point, this was just a bag of {flour|小麦粉}.
**Pete:** It was always just a bag of flour.
**Anna:** Pete, will you {listen|聞く/傾聴する} to yourself?
**Pete:** You sound {crazy|狂った/おかしな}.
**Pete:** I sound crazy
**Pete:** This whole {experiment|実験} was crazy.
**Pete:** She was the one who {carried|運んだ/持ち運んだ} around and fed it and what
**Professor Bot:** So what have we {learned|学んだ}
**Professor Bot:** We've learned when to use {reflexive|再帰的な} pronouns and when not to.
**Anna:** Go to our website for more {information|情報}.
**Anna:** You can practice using {reflexive|再帰的な} pronouns in our comments section.
